meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't deploy apps. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Fix Deployment Issues (Beginner Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment errors. Learn how to use AI debugging with Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first student project successfully.
content.answer_block: Deployments fail due to environment mismatches, missing dependencies, or misconfigured build steps. By leveraging AI-driven debugging tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can analyze logs, identify root causes, and generate production-ready fixes in seconds, transforming frustrating deployment errors into simple, actionable coding tasks.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting your code, perfecting the UI, and feeling the rush of a local build that actually works. Then, you hit 'Deploy'. Instead of a live link, you are met with a wall of red text: cryptic stack traces and 'Build Failed' notifications that feel like a dead end. It is the rite of passage every developer fears, but it doesn't have to be the end of your project.

This tutorial bridges the gap between local success and production reality. We aren't just fixing bugs; we are teaching you the professional workflow of using AI to diagnose infrastructure issues. By the end of this guide, you will stop fearing the console and start seeing deployment errors for what they really are: solvable puzzles.

steps.title: Isolate the Environment Context
steps.description: Before fixing, you must define the environment. AI needs to know your runtime, framework, and build platform to give accurate advice.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project structure and identify potential environment mismatches between my local development setup and a standard production build.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./package.json ./Dockerfile --instruction 'Identify potential environment conflicts for production deployment.'

steps.title: Diagnose Build Logs with AI
steps.description: Don't stare at logs blindly. Feed the error output directly into your AI agent to extract the specific failing line.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Parse these build logs and identify the exact dependency or configuration causing the deployment to crash.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'cat build-logs.txt' --instruction 'Find the root cause of the build failure in this log file.'

steps.title: Auto-Generate Fixes
steps.description: Once the root cause is identified, have the AI generate the necessary configuration changes to resolve the issue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a code fix or configuration change for my production build file to resolve the dependency error identified.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit ./config.yaml --instruction 'Update build parameters to resolve dependency resolution errors.'

steps.title: Validate with Dry-Run
steps.description: Before pushing to production, verify the fix by running a local build simulation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a script or command to simulate a production build locally to verify the fix works.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npm run build' --instruction 'Verify if the build succeeds with the latest changes.'

steps.title: Final Deployment Verification
steps.description: Ensure your environment variables are correctly mapped for the live environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my production deployment configuration and ensure all secrets and environment variables are properly mapped.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read .env.example --instruction 'Compare my current environment configuration against best practices for production.'

pro_tips: Always treat your build logs as structured data; copy them into a text file before passing them to Claude Code to avoid token window clutter.
Use 'Environment Parity' by creating a Dockerfile that mirrors your production server, allowing you to catch errors before they hit the cloud.
When debugging, ask the AI to explain 'why' a fix works, not just provide the code, to accelerate your long-term learning.
Keep your .gitignore updated; deployment errors are often caused by accidentally committing local configuration files that conflict with production.
Leverage the 'Claude Code' context window to index your entire repository; it can find hidden circular dependencies that logs often miss.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, focus on setting up a CI/CD pipeline to automate your testing and build process, ensuring every future push is verified by your AI agents automatically.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder who knows how to navigate the complexities of production. With AI as your co-pilot, no error is insurmountable. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
